title: Daily Market Digest: May 13, 2026
subtitle: Market closes mixed amid inflation concerns and geopolitical tensions.

items.headline: U.S. Markets Mixed as Inflation Data and Geopolitics Weigh
items.summary: U.S. stock markets closed mixed on Tuesday, May 12, with the Dow edging up while the S&P 500 and Nasdaq retreated from recent highs. This reaction followed the release of hotter-than-expected April CPI data and ongoing geopolitical conflicts in the Middle East. Early Wednesday, stock futures were mixed ahead of further inflation data.

items.statContext: Down 0.1% (-0.2% by some reports) on Tuesday. Futures up 0.23% pre-market Wednesday.
Down 0.7% on Tuesday. Futures up 0.75% pre-market Wednesday.
Up 0.1% on Tuesday. Futures down 0.21% pre-market Wednesday.

sections.title: Top Movers (Tuesday's Close / Early Wednesday Pre-Market)

items.headline: UnitedHealth Group Inc. leads Dow gainers
items.summary: UnitedHealth Group Inc. ($UNH) saw its stock price climb 3.1% at Tuesday's close, making it a major gainer in the blue-chip index.

items.headline: Zebra Technologies leaps on strong earnings
items.summary: Zebra Technologies ($ZBRA) saw its stock surge 11.4% after the company reported better-than-expected earnings results.

items.headline: Nvidia ($NVDA) gains in pre-market
items.summary: Nvidia shares are up another $3.12 in pre-market trading, continuing strong momentum for chip stocks.

items.headline: Intel ($INTC) slumps amid AI stock pullback
items.summary: Intel ($INTC) slumped 6.8% on Tuesday as chip companies and other AI-related stocks experienced sharp drops.

items.headline: Hims & Hers ($HIMS) misses Q1 expectations
items.summary: Hims & Hers Health, Inc. ($HIMS) slumped 15% after missing first-quarter expectations.

items.headline: Dream Finders Homes ($DFH) declines after rejected buyout bid
items.summary: Dream Finders Homes ($DFH) dropped 13.4% after its unsolicited buyout offer for Beazer Homes USA was rejected, with Beazer Homes also falling 7.3%.

items.headline: Wholesale Inflation Shocks Markets; PPI Report Due Today
items.summary: Wholesale inflation surged, with the Producer Price Index (PPI) jumping 1.4% in April, significantly higher than the 0.5% expected. Excluding food and energy, core PPI rose 1% against a 0.4% estimate. This follows Tuesday's hotter-than-expected Consumer Price Index (CPI) report, which saw headline CPI rise 0.6% in April. Investors are closely watching the PPI report scheduled for release today at 8:30 a.m. ET for further inflation insights.

items.headline: Fed Rate Hike Bets Increase Amid Persistent Inflation
items.summary: Rising Treasury yields indicate that traders anticipate the Federal Reserve will maintain high interest rates to combat inflation. Current market sentiment suggests a greater than 1-in-3 chance of a Fed rate hike by December, despite some analysts from Morgan Stanley suggesting the market does not require rate cuts for continued growth.

items.headline: Middle East Tensions Continue to Fuel Oil Price Concerns
items.summary: Geopolitical conflicts in the Middle East, particularly involving Iran and the United States, remain unresolved, contributing to market uncertainty. The conflict has reportedly led to a dramatic slowdown in shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil transit route, pushing Brent crude prices up 3.4% to $107.77 per barrel.

items.headline: Energy and Industrials Lead, Tech Mixed After AI Stock Pullback
items.summary: On Tuesday, the Energy Select Sector SPDR ($XLE) led gains with a 2.6% rise, followed by Industrials ($XLI) and Materials ($XLB), both up 1.3%. Information Technology ($XLK) also rose 1.1%. In contrast, Communication Services ($XLC) and Consumer Staples ($XLP) lagged, declining 1.2% and 1% respectively. The tech sector saw mixed performance, with large-cap names like Alphabet, Amazon, Microsoft, and Tesla down over 1%, while Nvidia and Apple posted modest gains. Chip stocks, including Intel, Micron, Broadcom, and AMD, experienced a pullback due to policy concerns and profit-taking in the AI boom.

items.headline: Earnings Season Continues: Eos Energy, Protalix, Beasley Broadcast Report
items.summary: Several companies reported Q1 2026 earnings today, May 13. Eos Energy Enterprises ($EOSE) announced a significant 445% year-over-year revenue increase. Protalix BioTherapeutics ($PLX) reaffirmed its 2026 revenue guidance, and Beasley Broadcast Group ($BBGI) also released its first-quarter results. Additionally, UFP Technologies ($UFPT) reported revenues in line with expectations and a modestly higher profit.
